name: Brothy Spinach and Peas Pasta
author: Kay Chun
category: Pastas, Dinner, Main Course, Lunch, Kid-Friendly
keywords: Pasta, Carrot, Spinach, Pea, Black-Eyed Pea, Stovetop, Easy, One Pot, Quick, Weeknight, Frozen Pea, Budget
description: This comforting vegetarian pasta is loaded with fresh spinach, peas and carrots in a light garlicky broth. Creamy protein-dense black-eyed peas provide meaty texture and enthusiastic stirring at the end of cooking helps the starches in the pasta lightly thicken the sauce. A finish of butter and grated Parmesan enriches the broth, while fresh grassy dill adds brightness to the dish. Serve with crusty bread to mop everything up.
ingredients: ½ medium white onion, finely chopped (½ cup)
5 cups low-sodium vegetable broth (or a combination of broth and water)
1 ¼ cups ditalini or other small pasta (6 ounces)
1 cup fresh or thawed frozen peas (5 ounces)
1 (15-ounce) can black eyed peas, rinsed and drained
1½ ounces Parmesan, finely grated (½ cup), plus more for serving
¼ cup coarsely chopped fresh dill
Crusty bread and red pepper flakes (optional), for serving
steps: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat oil over medium. Add onion, season with salt and pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 3 minutes. Add garlic and stir until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add broth, season with salt and pepper and bring to a boil over medium-high heat.

Add ditalini and carrots and cook according to package directions until pasta is a few minutes shy of al dente. Add spinach, fresh peas and black eyed peas and cook, stirring, until spinach is wilted and peas start to soften, about 2 minutes. Add butter and stir constantly, until pasta is al dente and sauce is slightly thickened but brothy, about 2 minutes. Turn off the heat. Stir in cheese and dill until well incorporated and season with salt and pepper.
Divide pasta and broth among bowls and top with more cheese, black pepper and red pepper flakes, if using. Serve warm with bread on the side.
